title: Frankenstein by Kate Hawley: Oscar Costume Design 2026
text: Kate Hawley has secured major recognition for her costume design work on Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein, winning both the Costume Designer's Guild award for Excellence in Costume Design for Period Film and a BAFTA. The acclaimed designer is now positioned as a strong contender for the Academy Award. Rather than adhering to traditional Victorian aesthetics, Hawley reimagined the 1850s-set story with vibrant jewel tones and elaborate detailing. Director del Toro requested bolder, more theatrical styling, particularly in the veils worn by characters like Elizabeth and Claire. Elizabeth's wardrobe drew inspiration from multiple sources. The colors derived from beetle specimens kept in the design workroom where they didn't use black but instead used purples, greens and blues layered together to achieve depth and richness. Her gowns incorporated marbled patterns reminiscent of Victorian book bindings, reflecting her intellectual interests. Necklines echoed those worn by Mary Shelley in historical portraits. Her wedding dress featured five layers of organza with a Swiss ribbon bodice, designed to visually connect her character to the creature through mirrored design elements. Jacob Elordi's creature initially wore only bandages before acquiring a Crimean War soldier's coat carrying the memory of that man embedded into it in the form of the outline of his spine.
